prevalence.population: Infants in the first year of life
prevalence.trend: stable; improved diagnosis and structured dietary management

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; nutrition and caregiver burden (specialized formulas)
burden.mortalityRate: Very low; anaphylaxis uncommon but possible

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical history plus elimination and supervised re-challenge; specific IgE/skin testing for IgE-mediated disease; rule out intolerance and other GI causes

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands of infants across the EU
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with appropriate formula access and dietetic follow-up

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Allergy services and oral food challenges available in many tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Cow’s milk elimination; eHF/AAF formulas when needed; dietitian support; planned supervised re-introduction to test tolerance
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Variation in reimbursement for specialized formulas and access to pediatric allergy in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Structured allergy care pathways and dietitian support; good access to hypoallergenic formulas in many systems
